SOUTH LYON MEDICAL CENTER

CCN: 295011 · YERINGTON, NV 89447 · Lyon County

ℹ Hospital-based

Overview

Address
213 WHITACRE ST, YERINGTON, NV 89447
Phone
7754632301
Certified beds
49
Avg daily residents
28 (57% of beds filled)
Ownership
Non-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1967-01-15
Setting
Urban
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 1/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 4.74 3.12 5.87 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.84 0.55 1.05 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.77 0.69 0.96 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 3.12 1.88 3.87 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.62 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.00 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
4.10
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.72
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
5.08
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
48.7%
annual
RN turnover
66.7%
annual
Administrators departed
New facility — insufficient data.

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.10
near national average
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.80
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-07-24 11 11 0 48 1 48
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-07-23 27 24 3 188 1 188
Total weighted health score
83.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (52)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

1 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
51 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-07-24 2025-07-30
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. D 2025-07-24 2025-07-30
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2025-07-24 2025-08-25
0727 Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. D 2025-07-24 2025-08-12
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. D 2025-07-24 2025-08-25
0838 Conduct and document a facility-wide assessment to determine what resources are necessary to care for residents competently during both day-to-day operations (including nights and weekends) and emergencies. D 2025-07-24 2025-08-25
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2025-07-24 2025-08-25
0865 Have a plan that describes the process for conducting QAPI and QAA activities. D 2025-07-24 2025-08-25
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2025-07-24 2025-08-26
0881 Implement a program that monitors antibiotic use. D 2025-07-24 2025-08-26
0882 Designate a qualified infection preventionist to be responsible for the infection prevent and control program in the nursing home. E 2025-07-24 2025-08-15
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2025-02-03 2025-03-17
0835 Administer the facility in a manner that enables it to use its resources effectively and efficiently. F 2025-02-03 2025-03-13
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2025-02-03 2025-03-17
0575 Post a list of names, addresses, and telephone numbers of all pertinent State agencies and advocacy groups and a statement that the resident may file a complaint with the State Survey Agency. D 2024-07-23 2024-08-27
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2024-07-23 2024-08-30
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2024-07-23 2024-08-30
0712 Ensure that the resident and his/her doctor meet face-to-face at all required visits. D 2024-07-23 2024-07-30
0730 Observe each nurse aide's job performance and give regular training. D 2024-07-23 2024-08-28
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2024-07-23 2024-07-23
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2024-07-23 2024-08-30
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2024-07-23 2024-08-30
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. D 2024-07-23 2024-08-01
0806 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food that accommodates resident allergies, intolerances, and preferences, as well as appealing options. D 2024-07-23 2024-08-01
0835 Administer the facility in a manner that enables it to use its resources effectively and efficiently. F 2024-07-23 2024-08-30
0865 Have a plan that describes the process for conducting QAPI and QAA activities. D 2024-07-23 2024-08-30
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2024-07-23 2024-08-30
0881 Implement a program that monitors antibiotic use. F 2024-07-23 2024-08-30
0882 Designate a qualified infection preventionist to be responsible for the infection prevent and control program in the nursing home. F 2024-07-23 2024-08-30
0883 Develop and implement policies and procedures for flu and pneumonia vaccinations. F 2024-07-23 2024-08-30
0887 Educate residents and staff on COVID-19 vaccination, offer the COVID-19 vaccine to eligible residents and staff after education, and properly document each resident and staff member's vaccination status. D 2024-07-23 2024-08-27
0941 Develop, implement, and/or maintain an effective training program that includes effective communications for direct care staff members. D 2024-07-23 2024-07-29
0942 Ensure that staff members are educated on resident rights and facility responsibilities to properly care for its residents. D 2024-07-23 2024-07-28
0943 Give their staff education on dementia care, and what abuse, neglect, and exploitation are; and how to report abuse, neglect, and exploitation. D 2024-07-23 2024-08-25
0944 Conduct mandatory training, for all staff, on the facility’s Quality Assurance and Performance Improvement Program. D 2024-07-23 2024-08-26
0945 Include as part of its infection prevention and control program, mandatory training that includes written standards, policies, and procedures for the program. D 2024-07-23 2024-07-29
0946 Provide training in compliance and ethics. D 2024-07-23 2024-07-30
0949 Provide behavior health training consistent with the requirements and as determined by a facility assessment. D 2024-07-23 2024-07-30
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2023-09-26 2023-10-16
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2023-09-26 2023-10-11
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted D 2023-09-26 2023-10-31
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2023-09-26 2023-10-31
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2023-09-26 2023-10-31
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2023-09-26 2023-10-31
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2023-09-26 2023-10-16
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. C 2023-09-26 2023-10-31
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2023-09-26 2023-10-31
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2023-09-26 2023-10-18
0835 Administer the facility in a manner that enables it to use its resources effectively and efficiently. D 2023-09-26 2023-10-31
0865 Have a plan that describes the process for conducting QAPI and QAA activities. D 2023-09-26 2023-10-11
